title: dianthus pelviformis
intro: Dianthus pelviformis, also known as the Fringed Pink, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is a perennial herb with pink or white flowers that have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to aid in digestion and relieve flatulence. The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Dianthus pelviformis can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support overall well-being and promote digestive health.
